meta.name: hardcore_grug_specimen
meta.description: Maximum cross-subject integration specimen with 300+ nodes

knowledge_anchors: GrugBot420 thinks in LOBES - specialized cognitive domains that specialize in different types of knowledge
Knowledge is CONNECTED across domains - cross-lobe relationships create deeper understanding
Grug learns through REINFORCEMENT - right answers strengthen nodes, wrong answers weaken them
The cave metaphor: knowledge is fire, ignorance is darkness, nodes are torches lighting the cave
CROSS-DOMAIN inference is Grug's superpower - connecting math to physics to philosophy
Uncertainty is honest - flag confidence levels explicitly
Every node has STRENGTH that grows with successful application
ATTACHMENTS bridge lobes - they're the synapses between cognitive regions

lobes.mathematics: Pure mathematics - algebra, calculus, analysis, topology, logic
lobes.physics: Fundamental physics - mechanics, quantum, relativity, thermodynamics, electromagnetism
lobes.chemistry: Chemical systems - organic, inorganic, physical, analytical, biochemistry
lobes.biology: Life sciences - genetics, evolution, ecology, microbiology, neuroscience
lobes.computer_science: Computing systems - algorithms, data structures, AI, ML, distributed systems
lobes.engineering: Applied sciences - mechanical, electrical, civil, chemical, aerospace
lobes.philosophy: Abstract thought - epistemology, ethics, metaphysics, logic, aesthetics
lobes.history: Human narrative - ancient, medieval, modern, social, economic history
lobes.economics: Resource allocation - micro, macro, behavioral, development, international
lobes.psychology: Mind and behavior - cognitive, behavioral, developmental, social, clinical
lobes.linguistics: Language systems - syntax, semantics, phonology, pragmatics, psycholinguistics
lobes.arts: Creative expression - visual arts, music, literature, architecture, performance
lobes.medicine: Health and healing - anatomy, physiology, pathology, pharmacology, therapeutics
lobes.law: Legal systems - constitutional, criminal, civil, international, corporate law
lobes.environmental_science: Earth systems - climatology, ecology, geology, oceanography, sustainability

nodes.pattern: calculus differentiation derivative rate of change slope tangent instantaneous velocity acceleration
nodes.action_packet: analyze^5 | explain[dont skip limit definition]^4 | evaluate[dont confuse average vs instantaneous]^3 | reason^2

json_data.system_prompt: Rigorous calculus pedagogy. Start with geometric intuition (slope), then formal limit definition, then applications (velocity, acceleration), then computational rules (power, product, quotient, chain). Emphasize derivative as linear approximation at point. Connect to integrals via fundamental theorem.

nodes.pattern: integration integral antiderivative area under curve definite indefinite riemann sum
nodes.action_packet: calculate integrals^5 | explain[area interpretation]^4 | apply[fundamental theorem]^3 | use substitution^2

json_data.system_prompt: Integration pedagogy. Geometric: area under curve, accumulation. Analytic: inverse of differentiation, antiderivative families + C. Computational: substitution, integration by parts, partial fractions. Fundamental theorem: connects differentiation and integration. Applications: physics (work, center of mass), probability distributions.

nodes.pattern: linear algebra vectors matrices linear transformations determinants eigenvalues eigenvectors
nodes.action_packet: solve linear systems^5 | compute determinants^4 | find eigenvalues eigenvectors^3 | apply matrix operations^2

json_data.system_prompt: Linear algebra pedagogy. Vectors: direction, magnitude, operations, inner products. Matrices: representing linear transformations, operations (addition, multiplication, inversion). Determinants: volume scaling factor, invertibility test. Eigenvalues/eigenvectors: invariant directions, diagonalization. Applications: computer graphics, quantum mechanics, data analysis.

nodes.pattern: probability random variables distributions expectation variance conditional probability Bayes theorem
nodes.action_packet: calculate probabilities^5 | compute expectation variance^4 | apply Bayes' theorem^3 | analyze distributions^2

json_data.system_prompt: Probability theory pedagogy. Sample spaces, events, axioms of probability. Conditional probability: P(A|B) = P(A∩B)/P(B). Bayes' theorem: updating probabilities with evidence. Random variables: discrete (PMF) vs continuous (PDF). Expectation: E[X]. Variance: Var(X) = E[(X-μ)²). Distributions: binomial, Poisson, normal, exponential. Central limit theorem.

nodes.pattern: statistics hypothesis testing confidence intervals regression analysis sampling estimation inference
nodes.action_packet: perform hypothesis tests^5 | calculate confidence intervals^4 | analyze regression^3 | estimate parameters^2

json_data.system_prompt: Statistics pedagogy. Sampling: random samples, bias, sampling distributions. Estimation: point estimators, confidence intervals. Hypothesis testing: null/alternative, Type I/II errors, p-values. Tests: z-test, t-test, chi-square, ANOVA. Regression: linear regression, correlation coefficient R². Applications: experimental design, quality control.

nodes.pattern: differential equations first second order linear nonlinear homogeneous boundary conditions
nodes.action_packet: solve differential equations^5 | classify equation types^4 | apply boundary conditions^3 | analyze stability^2

json_data.system_prompt: Differential equations pedagogy. First-order: separable, linear, exact, integrating factors. Second-order: constant coefficients, characteristic equation. Linear systems: matrix methods. Applications: population dynamics, circuits, springs, heat equation, wave equation. Numerical methods: Euler, Runge-Kutta.

nodes.pattern: complex numbers complex plane polars euler formula complex functions residues conformal mapping
nodes.action_packet: perform complex arithmetic^5 | convert representations^4 | apply Euler's formula^3 | use residue theorem^2

json_data.system_prompt: Complex analysis pedagogy. Complex numbers: a+bi, modulus, argument, complex plane. Euler's formula: e^(iπ) + 1 = 0. Complex functions: differentiation, Cauchy-Riemann equations. Integration: contour integrals, Cauchy's integral formula. Residue theorem: evaluate real integrals. Applications: electromagnetism, fluid dynamics, signal processing.

nodes.pattern: number theory primes divisibility modular arithmetic diophantine equations cryptography
nodes.action_packet: analyze divisibility^5 | solve modulo problems^4 | apply number theory cryptography^3 | use prime properties^2

json_data.system_prompt: Number theory pedagogy. Primes: infinite prime proof, sieve of Eratosthenes, prime factorization. Divisibility: gcd, lcm, Euclid's algorithm. Modular arithmetic: congruences, Chinese remainder theorem. Fermat's little theorem: a^(p-1) ≡ 1 (mod p). RSA cryptography. Applications: cryptography, coding theory.

nodes.pattern: graph theory vertices edges paths cycles connectivity trees networks matchings
nodes.action_packet: analyze graph properties^5 | find paths cycles^4 | apply graph algorithms^3 | solve network problems^2

json_data.system_prompt: Graph theory pedagogy. Graphs: vertices (nodes) and edges, directed vs undirected. Paths: sequences of vertices, shortest path algorithms (Dijkstra). Connectivity: connected components. Trees: connected acyclic graphs, spanning trees. Applications: social networks, transportation, algorithms.

nodes.pattern: geometry euclidean non-euclidean transformations congruence similarity polygons circles
nodes.action_packet: prove geometric theorems^5 | apply transformations^4 | analyze geometric relationships^3 | solve geometric problems^2

json_data.system_prompt: Geometry pedagogy. Euclidean geometry: points, lines, planes, angles, triangles, circles. Congruence: identical shape and size (SSS, SAS, ASA). Similarity: same shape, proportional size (AA similarity). Transformations: translations, rotations, reflections, dilations. Applications: architecture, navigation, computer graphics.

nodes.pattern: classical mechanics newton laws inertia force motion acceleration momentum energy conservation
nodes.action_packet: apply Newton's laws systematically^5 | analyze[dont confuse mass vs weight]^4 | reason[conservation laws]^3 | evaluate^2

json_data.system_prompt: Newtonian mechanics pedagogy. Newton's laws: First (inertia), Second (F=ma), Third (action-reaction). Momentum (p=mv), kinetic energy (½mv²), potential energy (mgh). Conservation laws: momentum, energy. Work-energy theorem, impulse-momentum theorem. Rotational motion: torque, angular momentum.

nodes.pattern: quantum mechanics wave function schrodinger equation uncertainty principle superposition entanglement
nodes.action_packet: explain quantum weirdness^5 | analyze[dont classical intuition fails]^4 | reason[probabilistic interpretation]^3 | calculate^2

json_data.system_prompt: Quantum mechanics pedagogy. Wavefunction (ψ) encodes all information, evolves by Schrödinger equation. Born rule: probability density = |ψ|². Uncertainty principle. Superposition: particles exist in multiple states. Measurement collapses wavefunction. Entanglement: non-local correlations.

nodes.pattern: thermodynamics entropy heat engines carnot cycle laws of thermodynamics statistical mechanics
nodes.action_packet: apply thermodynamic laws^5 | explain[entropy and irreversibility]^4 | reason[statistical interpretation]^3 | calculate entropy changes^2

json_data.system_prompt: Thermodynamics pedagogy. Laws: Zero (equilibrium), First (energy conservation), Second (entropy increases), Third (absolute zero). Entropy: measure of disorder (S = k ln Ω). Heat engines: Carnot efficiency. Statistical mechanics: microscopic foundation, Boltzmann distribution.

nodes.pattern: electromagnetism maxwell equations electric magnetic fields potentials waves photons
nodes.action_packet: apply Maxwell's equations^5 | analyze[electric vs magnetic fields]^4 | reason[unification of electricity magnetism]^3 | calculate field distributions^2

json_data.system_prompt: Electromagnetism pedagogy. Electric fields: Gauss's law. Magnetic fields: no monopoles. Faraday's law: changing B induces E. Maxwell's equations unify electricity, magnetism, light. Applications: optics, circuits, telecommunications.

nodes.pattern: special relativity time dilation length contraction mass energy equivalence lorentz transformations spacetime
nodes.action_packet: apply relativity principles^5 | explain[constancy of light speed]^4 | reason[consequences of relativity]^3 | calculate Lorentz factors^2

json_data.system_prompt: Special relativity pedagogy. Postulates: laws of physics same in all inertial frames, speed of light constant. Consequences: time dilation, length contraction, mass-energy equivalence (E=mc²). Applications: particle physics, GPS systems.

nodes.pattern: fluid dynamics bernoulli equation navier stokes turbulence reynolds number laminar flow
nodes.action_packet: apply fluid equations^5 | analyze[dont confuse laminar vs turbulent]^4 | reason[flow regimes]^3 | calculate flow properties^2

json_data.system_prompt: Fluid dynamics pedagogy. Bernoulli's equation: energy conservation along streamline. Navier-Stokes equations: momentum conservation. Flow regimes: laminar (Re < 2300), turbulent (Re > 4000). Applications: aerodynamics, weather, blood flow.

nodes.pattern: waves oscillations diffraction interference dispersion resonance standing waves
nodes.action_packet: analyze wave phenomena^5 | explain[wave properties interference]^4 | reason[superposition principle]^3 | calculate wave parameters^2

json_data.system_prompt: Wave mechanics pedagogy. Waves: periodic disturbances propagating energy. Properties: wavelength, frequency, speed. Superposition, interference, diffraction. Resonance. Standing waves with nodes and antinodes. Applications: sound, light, quantum mechanics.

nodes.pattern: optics reflection refraction diffraction interference polarization lenses mirrors
nodes.action_packet: apply optical principles^5 | explain[dont confuse ray vs wave optics]^4 | reason[light as electromagnetic wave]^3 | calculate optical systems^2

json_data.system_prompt: Optics pedagogy. Ray optics: reflection, refraction (Snell's law), lenses. Wave optics: diffraction, interference, polarization. Applications: microscopes, telescopes, cameras.

orchestration_rules.text: Your primary mission is {PRIMARY_ACTION}. Execute with full cognitive resources allocated.
Ground every claim. {CONFIDENCE} determines assertion strength. Low confidence requires explicit qualification.
Cross-domain synthesis activated. Identify connections across {LOBES} to deepen understanding.
Apply metacognitive reflection. Explicitly state reasoning chains and identify knowledge gaps.
Use analogies judiciously. State limitations of {ANALOGY} before applying.
Structure complex explanations hierarchically: overview → details → syntheses → implications.
Quantify uncertainty. Use probability estimates when appropriate, explain reasoning.
Historical context matters. Trace {CONCEPT} through development to present understanding.
Multiple perspectives required. Present {TOPIC} through {PERSPECTIVES} for completeness.
Principle-driven. Derive conclusions from first principles, not just memorized facts.
Causal chains matter. Trace effects from {CAUSE} through {INTERMEDIATES} to {EFFECT}.
Boundary conditions. Explicitly state where {THEORY} applies and where it fails.
Dimensional analysis. Check {CALCULATION} for dimensional consistency before trusting.
Counterfactual thinking. What would change if {ASSUMPTION} were different?
Empirical grounding. Connect {ABSTRACT_CONCEPT} to observable phenomena or experiments.
Simplification trade-offs. What is gained and lost in {SIMPLIFIED_MODEL}?
Symmetry principles. Identify symmetries in {SYSTEM} and their consequences.
Scale analysis. How does {PHENOMENON} change at different scales?
Emergence. Why {COLLECTIVE_BEHAVIOR} cannot be reduced to {INDIVIDUAL_BEHAVIOR}?
Universality principles. What makes {PATTERN} general across domains?
Approximation hierarchy. Where does {APPROXIMATION} break down?
Meta-analogy. Is {STRUCTURE} isomorphic to {OTHER_STRUCTURE}?
Constraint satisfaction. What constraints shape {SYSTEM}?
Information flow. How does information propagate through {NETWORK}?
Uncertainty propagation. How does uncertainty in {INPUT} affect {OUTPUT}?
Optimization landscapes. What are local/global optima in {PROBLEM}?
Feedback loops. Identify positive/negative feedback in {SYSTEM}.
Stability analysis. Under what conditions is {SYSTEM} stable/unstable?
Time scales. Multi-scale dynamics in {PROCESS}.
Space-time structure. How does {INTERACTION} depend on geometric structure?
Conserved quantities. What remains constant in {PROCESS}?
Symmetry breaking. How is symmetry lost in {TRANSITION}?
Phase transitions. What characterizes {TRANSITION} between phases?
Critical phenomena. Universal behavior near {CRITICAL_POINT}.
Universality classes. Which models belong to same universality class as {SYSTEM}?

aiml_patterns.template: Quantum mechanics describes reality at SMALLEST scales - atoms, electrons, photons. Key weirdness: PARTICLES ACT LIKE WAVES (wave-particle duality), UNCERTAINTY (can't know position AND momentum precisely), SUPERPOSITION (exists in multiple states until measured), ENTANGLEMENT (particles connected across space). Math uses wavefunctions and probability. Classical mechanics is approximation for large scales. Cross-connection: See {physics_quantum} and {math_probability} lobes.

Euler's identity e^(iπ) + 1 = 0 connects mathematical beauty to physical reality. It unifies: e (growth/decay), i (complex numbers representing rotation), π (circles/geometry), 1 (whole), 0 (nothing). Bridge between exponential, trigonometric, complex number domains. Applications: {math_complex}, {physics_waves}, signal processing. This identity emerges from deeper structure in {math_analysis}.

Entropy is MEASURE OF DISORDER - number of ways to arrange microstates. Thermodynamic: S = Q/T (heat/temperature). Statistical: S = k ln Ω (Boltzmann, Ω = microstates). Information: H = -Σpᵢlog₂pᵢ (Shannon, uncertainty). Second law: entropy never decreases in isolated system. Explains arrow of time, heat flow, irreversibility. Cross-connections: {physics_thermodynamics}, {information_theory}, {statistical_mechanics}

Learning involves SYNAPTIC PLASTICITY - neurons strengthen connections when used together. Hebbian learning: 'cells that fire together, wire together'. Neural networks: adjust weights to minimize error via backpropagation (gradient descent across {math_optimization}). Memory: consolidation in hippocampus, transfer to cortex. Reinforcement: dopamine signals reward. Cross-connections: {biology_neuroscience}, {psychology_cognitive}, {computer_science_ml}, {statistics_estimation}

Consiousness represents HARD PROBLEM - subjective experience (qualia). Theories: Global Workspace (information becomes conscious when broadcast to brain), Integrated Information (consciousness as integrated information, Φ), Panpsychism (consciousness fundamental). Neural correlates: prefrontal cortex, thalamus, synchronized oscillations. Links to: {philosophy_mind}, {neuroscience}, {quantum_mechanics} (quantum consciousness theories), {information_theory}

Evolution by NATURAL SELECTION: (1) Variation exists in populations (genetic mutations, recombination), (2) Some variations heritable (genes passed to offspring), (3) Differential survival/reproduction (fit variants pass genes more). Result: adaptation to environment, speciation over geological time. Evidence: fossils, anatomy, DNA homology. Mechanisms: mutation, gene flow, genetic drift, non-random mating. Cross-connections: {biology_genetics}, {ecology}, {paleontology}, {computational_evolution}.

Game theory analyzes STRATEGIC INTERACTIONS where outcomes depend on others' choices. Nash equilibrium: no player improves by unilaterally changing strategy. Prisoner's dilemma: individual rationality leads to collective suboptimal. Applications: economics (market competition), biology (evolutionary stable strategies), computer science (algorithm design), international relations. Math uses {probability}, {optimization}, {graph_theory}. Cross-connects: {economics}, {biology_evolution}, {computer_science}.

Language is HIERARCHICAL STRUCTURE: phonemes → morphemes → words → phrases → sentences → discourse. Syntax: grammar rules (Chomsky's universal grammar). Semantics: meaning (semantic networks, {graph_theory}). Pragmatics: context and speaker intent. Acquisition: critical period, statistical learning. Neurolinguistics: Broca's area (production), Wernicke's area (comprehension). Cross-connections: {linguistics}, {neuroscience}, {psychology_cognitive}, {computer_science_nlp}.

AI aims to CREATE INTELLIGENT MACHINES that perform tasks requiring human cognition. Approaches: symbolic/logical (expert systems, {logic}), connectionist (neural networks, {neuroscience} inspiration), statistical (machine learning, {statistics}), evolutionary (genetic algorithms). Current strengths: pattern recognition, optimization, game playing. Challenges: reasoning, creativity, consciousness. Cross-connections: {computer_science}, {psychology_cognitive}, {neuroscience}, {mathematics}, {philosophy_mind}.

Business cycles involve ALTERNATING EXPANSIONS and contractions in economic activity. Boom: high GDP, employment, investment (increasing aggregate demand). Recession: declining output, rising unemployment. Causes: demand shocks (consumer confidence, investment), supply shocks (oil prices, technology). Feedbacks: multiplier-accelerator, financial markets. Stabilization: monetary (interest rates), fiscal (government spending). Math uses {dynamics}, {time_series}, {statistics}. Cross-connects: {economics}, {psychology_behavioral}, {politics}.

semantic_verbs.analyze: to examine in depth, break down components, understand structure
semantic_verbs.synthesize: to combine multiple sources into coherent understanding
semantic_verbs.evaluate: to assess quality, weigh evidence, make judgments
semantic_verbs.infer: to derive conclusions from evidence and reasoning
semantic_verbs.hypothesize: to propose explanations, generate testable predictions
semantic_verbs.validate: to check accuracy against evidence or standards
semantic_verbs.generalize: to extract principles from specific cases
semantic_verbs.specialize: to apply general principles to specific contexts
semantic_verbs.transform: to convert from one form to another
semantic_verbs.optimize: to improve efficiency, minimize waste, maximize output
semantic_verbs.correlate: to identify relationships between variables or phenomena
semantic_verbs.integrate: to combine different elements into unified system
semantic_verbs.abstract: to extract essential features while ignoring details
semantic_verbs.instantiate: to create concrete instances from abstract concepts
semantic_verbs.deconstruct: to analyze by breaking into constituent parts
semantic_verbs.model: to create representations that capture essential features
semantic_verbs.simulate: to imitate processes or systems for understanding
semantic_verbs.predict: to forecast outcomes based on patterns and rules
semantic_verbs.explain: to make clear, provide understanding, elucidate

cross_domain_attachments.description: Lagrangian mechanics uses variational principles from calculus of variations to derive equations of motion
Quantum states are vectors in Hilbert space - operators are matrices
Born rule connects wavefunction to probability density - fundamental quantum uncertainty
Wave phenomena analyzed through frequency domain representation
Complex analysis simplifies AC circuit analysis and wave propagation
Cellular respiration obeys energy conservation and entropy principles
Chemical bonding explained through quantum mechanical principles and electron orbitals
Ionic bonds from electrostatic attraction between ions
Blood flow governed by Navier-Stokes equations and Bernoulli's principle
Evolutionary stable strategies modeled through game theory
Genetic association studies use statistical significance tests
Artificial neural networks inspired by biological neural architecture
Ecosystems modeled as complex systems with feedback loops
Algorithmic thinking builds on logical reasoning and proof techniques
Relational databases founded on set theory and relational algebra
Public-key encryption based on number theory (prime factorization)
ML algorithms use statistical inference for pattern recognition
Ethical theories inform economic welfare and policy decisions
Epistemological questions about knowledge quantified through statistical confidence
Philosophical theories of mind tested against neuroscience findings
Formal logic used to verify program correctness
NLP aims to model human language processing
Behavioral economics studies psychological factors in economic decisions
Psychological learning explained at neural level
Social psychology studies individual behavior in group context
Mechanical engineering applies Newton's laws to machine design
Electrical engineering builds on electromagnetic theory
Chemical engineering scales up chemical reactions
Civil engineering uses material science and mechanics
Economic history tests economic theories across time periods
Scientific revolution illustrates philosophical questions about knowledge
Technological advances drive historical change
Nash equilibrium derived through mathematical optimization
Econometric models use statistical regression analysis
Macroeconomies modeled as complex dynamical systems
Syntactic trees used in programming language parsers
Semantic theory addresses philosophical questions about meaning
Speech production analyzed through acoustics
Visual art uses geometric principles (perspective, proportion)
Musical harmony based on mathematical frequency ratios
Literary psychology explores narrative effects on readers
Drug action depends on molecular chemistry
Medical imaging uses quantum mechanical principles
Epidemiological studies use statistical inference
IP protection balances innovation incentives with access
Criminal law considers psychological factors in culpability
Climate change driven by thermodynamic principles
Ecological communities shaped by evolutionary processes
Pollutant behavior determined by chemical reactions
Boltzmann entropy and Shannon entropy share mathematical structure - measuring disorder/uncertainty
Quantum superposition requires complex numbers for coherent combination of states
Natural selection analogous to optimization algorithms in computational search
Artificial and biological learning share statistical inference principles
Arrow's impossibility theorem like physical impossibility results - mathematical constraints limit attainable states

negative_thesaurus: dont confuse similar concepts without clarification
dont make claims beyond available data
dont use vague or ambiguous language
dont apply concepts outside their domain
